steps.title: Find the decisive clue
steps.content: The phrase “eventually forming new portions of land” tells us what the sediments’ action must lead to. The blank needs a word that describes sediments building up — an action that logically produces new land where the river meets the South China Sea.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong words and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each choice against the clue:
  • Choice A — accumulate: sediments accumulate — to collect and build up over time, which directly produces new land. ✓
  • Choice B — dissolve: sediments dissolve — to break apart into liquid; dissolved sediments cannot form new land. ✗
  • Choice C — accelerate: sediments accelerate — to speed up; speeding sediments do not explain the formation of new land. ✗
  • Choice D — thrive: sediments thrive — to flourish or prosper, a term for living things, not inorganic sediments. ✗
That is the correct answer.
